title: senna stipulacea var. stipulacea
intro: Senna stipulacea var. stipulacea is a plant species within the legume family, commonly known as the 'Kanuka' or 'Kanuka senna'. It is a medicinal plant used for its laxative properties, derived from its seeds, which contain anthraquinone glycosides. The medicinal properties of Senna stipulacea var. stipulacea have been traditionally used to treat constipation, as well as other gastrointestinal issues, and it also exhibits anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. This plant can be prepared as herbal teas, capsules, or powder, and its health benefits include promoting bowel movements, reducing symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), and supporting overall gut health.
